settings.text: The Fly Trap That Fills Up Fast

settings.benefit_2_text: Flies in the Bag Within 15 Minutes
settings.benefit_3_text: No Chemicals, No Power, No Refills
settings.benefit_4_text: Toss It When It's Full — Done

settings.custom_payment_icons: mastercard, american_express, apple_pay, google_pay, shopify_pay, discover, visa

settings.review_text: I ordered 3 to try and came back for 9 more. Can't believe how many flies it caught. Highly recommend!

settings.answer_1: Yes — and that's the whole point. The bait smells because flies are wired to hunt it down. Hang it 20 feet from your patio and you won't smell a thing. The flies will be too busy drowning in the bag to bother you.
settings.question_2: Won't it just bring more flies into my yard?
settings.answer_2: Opposite. It pulls flies away from where you sit and toward wherever you hang the trap. The trick is placement — hang it on the far fence, near the bins, or along the tree line. Your patio clears out within days.
settings.answer_3: Then you don't pay. 60-day money-back guarantee, no questions. But out of 30,000+ customers, the complaint we hear most isn't "it didn't work." It's "the bag filled up too fast."
settings.question_4: How long does one bag last?
settings.answer_4: Depends on your fly situation. Farms and ranches fill a bag in 3-7 days. Suburban yards typically get 2-4 weeks. Either way, if it's filling fast — that means it's working. Most customers grab the 6-pack so they can rotate fresh bags all season without running out.

settings.step_description: Fill the bag to activate the bait. That's all the setup you'll ever need.
Hang at least 20 feet from your patio or door. The farther from where you sit, the better.
When it's full, throw it away. No cleaning, no refills, no touching dead flies.

settings.title: Sprays Wear Off. Strips Catch 3 Flies. Zappers Change Nothing.

settings.subtitle: You've spent more money on things that don't work than you want to admit. FlyDrop catches more flies in a single afternoon than most products catch in a month. One bag. Thousands of flies. No more guessing.

settings.content: I have been dealing with flies! We are rural and there is a chicken farm about 1.5 miles away. It's rough. Trust me. I was complaining to a neighbor and she said to get the fly bags. OMG! I thought they weren't going to work. Wrong. They are amazing. Over the last 2 months, our house has been nearly fly-free. It's a game changer.

I live in Southern Alabama and the flies are a plenty. I have dogs and dog poop just attracts flies, no matter how fast you scoop the poop. I've tried everything to help reduce the flies and this is the only fly catcher that works.

I set one of these up in my yard, close enough so I could see it, but not smell it. After just a few minutes there it had flies all over. After two days, hundreds of them are in the water. I've not had a problem with them on my deck since I put this up.

These things stink like crazy so do not hang close to the house. It does however solve a lot of the summer fly problem we have in the south. So the smell is awful, but the flies love it. You have to pick the battle for yourself.

I live near a horse pasture and have chickens too. Flies abound in my world, hence the traps. They work! They work so well! Works well on blue bottle flies and house flies. Buy these traps. Wage war on the flies.

We've never had a big fly problem until some cows were added to the field behind us. We were overrun with flies. I put up 2 of these along our fence line and within hours there were hundreds of flies in each of them. These will be a new addition to the yard every year.

We camp all summer and of course the flies do too. We had tried many types and brands of fly deterrents but they weren't much help. You just add water and hang them up. To remove it, just take it down, screw down the top and toss it in the trash. You will be shocked how many flies it catches.
We have been using these for 4 summers and these keep the flies away from the folks swimming in our pool. They stink when full — but they are full. We take them down and put up a new set.

My husband and I purchased these fly traps to help cut down on some of the flies in our yard. These traps worked better than we expected. We were shocked to find we were filling up a bag every 1 to 2 days. We absolutely see a decrease in flies in our yard.
Don't waste your money on other products or do-it-yourself formulas and traps. Absolutely best product I've found and I've tried a bunch.

settings.question: Does this actually catch flies, or will it just sit there like every other trap I've tried?
settings.answer: We get it — you've bought the sprays that wore off in an hour, the strips that caught three flies, and the zapper that sounded impressive but changed nothing. FlyDrop uses a bait attractant that flies physically cannot resist. Most customers see flies entering the bag within 15-20 minutes of hanging it. 85% leave a 5-star review. And if it doesn't fill up, we'll refund every cent.

settings.question: How bad is the smell? Be honest.
settings.answer: Bad. That's what makes it work. The bait smells like something died — and that's exactly why flies rush into the bag instead of landing on your plate. The fix is simple: hang it 20+ feet from where you sit. At that distance you won't notice it. The flies will. Every single repeat customer says the same thing — "it stinks, but I'd take the smell over the flies any day."

settings.question: I have horses/chickens/dogs — will this actually make a dent?
settings.answer: This is where FlyDrop earns its reputation. Horse owners, chicken keepers, and farm operators are our most loyal customers — many have been reordering for years. One rancher told us she fills a bag every 3-4 days near her barn. It won't eliminate every fly on a working property, but it will cut the population dramatically and pull them away from your animals and your house.

settings.question: Will it just attract MORE flies to my yard?
settings.answer: This is the #1 concern we hear and it comes down to placement. FlyDrop pulls flies toward the trap and away from where you are. If you hang it next to your patio door, yes — you'll see more flies near your door. Hang it on the back fence, near the trash cans, or along the tree line and you'll watch your patio clear out. Placement is everything. 20 feet minimum.

settings.question: Which pack size do I need?
settings.answer: If you've got a standard suburban yard and want to test it, the 3-pack gets you started. But the 6-pack is what most customers wish they'd grabbed first — enough to cover multiple spots and replace bags as they fill. Got horses, chickens, or a large property? The 9-pack. Serious fly problems burn through bags fast and you don't want to run out mid-season.

settings.question: What if the bag leaks or doesn't work?
settings.answer: Email us. We replace it, no questions asked. No return shipping, no photos required, no runaround. We stand behind every bag because 30,000+ customers already proved this works. The rare dud doesn't change that — it just means we owe you a fresh one.

settings.question: I'll probably come back and order later.
settings.answer: Fly season doesn't pause while you think it over. The 40% off Spring Sale won't last. And if we're being real — every week you wait is another week of swatting flies off your food, chasing them out of the kitchen, and watching your dogs get tormented. You've read the reviews. You've seen the results. A bag costs less than a can of Raid and catches more flies in a day than a zapper does in a month. The guarantee means you risk nothing. The flies are already outside.
